ten_de: IELTS Academic Test - 3 Skills (Listening, Reading, Writing)
mo_ta: Đề thi IELTS Academic Test 3 kỹ năng: Listening, Reading, Writing

bai_tap.huong_dan: You will hear a conversation between two people about a hotel booking. Answer the questions below.

cau_hoi.nhan: What type of room does the customer want?
How many nights will they stay?

bai_tap.huong_dan: Fill in the blanks with the correct information from the recording.
Complete the notes below. Write TRUE if the statement matches the information, FALSE if it contradicts, or NOT GIVEN if there is no information.

cau_hoi.nhan: The university offers free parking for all students.
Library opening hours have been extended.

bai_tap.huong_dan: Match each person with the correct information about their course.

bai_tap.passage:
The Growth of Organic Farming

Organic farming has become increasingly popular over the past decade. This method of agriculture avoids the use of synthetic pesticides and chemical fertilizers. Instead, it relies on natural processes and techniques to maintain soil fertility.

Many farmers have converted to organic methods due to consumer demand for healthier food. However, organic farming typically requires more labor and produces lower yields compared to conventional farming. Despite these challenges, the environmental benefits make it an attractive option for sustainable agriculture.

The certification process for organic products is rigorous. Farmers must follow strict guidelines for at least three years before their products can be labeled as organic. This has led some to criticize the system as too restrictive.
bai_tap.huong_dan: Do the following statements agree with the information in the passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if it contradicts, or NOT GIVEN if there is no information.

cau_hoi.noi_dung: Organic farming uses only natural pesticides.
Organic farms always produce higher yields than conventional farms.
Certification takes at least three years to complete.
Organic farming requires less labor than conventional farming.
All farmers can label their products as organic immediately.

bai_tap.passage:
The History of the Internet

The internet began as a military project in the 1960s. The US Department of Defense wanted to create a network that could survive a nuclear attack. ARPA (Advanced Research Projects Agency) was tasked with developing this system.

In 1969, the first message was sent between two computers at UCLA and Stanford Research Institute. This marked the beginning of what we now call the internet. Throughout the 1970s and 1980s, the network expanded to include universities and research institutions.

The World Wide Web was invented in 1989 by Tim Berners-Lee, a British scientist at CERN. He created the system of hypertext links that made the internet accessible to ordinary users. By the mid-1990s, millions of people were using the web daily.


Today, the internet has transformed virtually every aspect of modern life, from communication to commerce to entertainment.
bai_tap.huong_dan: Choose the best answer (A, B, C, or D) for each question.

cau_hoi.noi_dung: Why was the internet originally created?
Who invented the World Wide Web?
When did the internet become popular with ordinary users?
What is the main topic of the passage?
According to the passage, what transformed modern life?

bai_tap.passage:
Climate Change Effects

Climate change is affecting ecosystems around the world. Polar ice caps are melting, causing sea levels to rise. This threatens coastal communities and island nations. Temperature increases are also causing shifts in animal migration patterns and plant growing seasons.

Scientists warn that without immediate action, these effects will worsen. The Paris Agreement aims to limit global temperature rise to 1.5 degrees Celsius. However, current emission levels suggest this target may be missed.


Renewable energy adoption is accelerating. Solar and wind power are now cost-competitive with fossil fuels. Electric vehicle sales are growing rapidly. These trends offer hope for reducing future climate impacts.
bai_tap.huong_dan: Complete the summary below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

cau_hoi.noi_dung: Polar ice caps are causing sea levels to ___
The Paris Agreement aims to limit temperature rise to ___ degrees.
Solar and ___ power are now cost-competitive.

bai_tap.huong_dan:
The bar chart below shows the number of books read by students in a university library in 2010, 2015, and 2020.

Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

Write at least 150 words.

cau_hoi.noi_dung: Task 1: Write a minimum of 150 words about the bar chart.

bai_tap.huong_dan:
Some people believe that university students should study whatever they like. Others think that they should only be allowed to study subjects that will be useful in the future, such as those related to science and technology.

Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.

Write at least 250 words.

cau_hoi.noi_dung: Task 2: Write an essay of at least 250 words (will be graded manually).
